# Sources for Music, Media, and Synthetic Content

- **DOJ — Michael Smith AI-assisted music streaming fraud guilty plea** (Law enforcement / primary). DOJ says Smith created hundreds of thousands of AI-generated songs and used bots to stream them billions of times to mimic genuine listening and steal royalty payments. URL: https://www.justice.gov/usao-sdny/pr/north-carolina-man-pleads-guilty-music-streaming-fraud-aided-artificial-intelligence-0
- **Deezer — AI-generated tracks represent 44% of new uploaded music** (Platform transparency / primary). Deezer says it receives almost 75,000 AI-generated tracks per day, about 44% of daily uploads; 85% of streams on fully AI-generated tracks were detected as fraudulent and demonetized in 2025. URL: https://newsroom-deezer.com/2026/04/ai-generated-tracks-represent-44-of-new-uploaded-music/
- **Vanity Fair — Influencers AI clones** (News / cultural reporting). Discusses influencer AI clones/digital twins, likeness control, consent, contract rights, fan trust and audience disclosure. URL: https://www.vanityfair.com/news/story/influencers-ai-clones
- **The Verge — AI influencers are everywhere at Coachella** (News / tech reporting). Reports AI-generated influencers around Coachella and disclosure concerns. URL: https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/911267/ai-influencers-coachella
- **WIRED — The Fanfare Around the Band Geese Actually Was a Psyop** (News / cultural reporting). Reports on Chaotic Good Projects, Geese, networks of social pages, TikTok recommendation-seeding, burner accounts/comments, and the phrase “trend simulation”. URL: https://www.wired.com/story/geese-chaotic-good-marketing-industry-plant/
